response.justification: The candidate has 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and low-latency domains, but has no meaningful overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. There is no evidence of SoC or IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD experience, deep neural network training or inference optimization, or hardware-software co-design at the silicon level. The AI-related work at re:cinq is software-layer tooling (MCP frameworks and agentic workflows), not AI accelerator hardware. The educational background is a BS in Computer Science with no graduate degree, and the entire career trajectory is in full-stack and systems software rather than hardware performance engineering.
strengths: Demonstrated low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, high-performance databases) at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second
Broad systems breadth spanning embedded, cloud infrastructure tooling, and developer platforms shows adaptability across technical layers
15+ years of consistent senior/staff-level delivery across diverse domains indicates strong engineering fundamentals
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator hardware — the core technical requirements of the role
No background in deep learning workload analysis, SIMD/MIMD architectures, or performance profiling of neural network training/inference pipelines
Educational profile (BS only) combined with a career path entirely in application/systems software does not meet the depth expected at principal IC level for this domain

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DNN optimization background
